summary: binding.gyp uses GYP command-expansion to execute shell at install time
details: The package ships a binding.gyp at the package root containing GYP command-expansion syntax (`<!(...)`) at line 6 within the targets/sources fields. npm implicitly runs `node-gyp rebuild` whenever a binding.gyp is present — even when no install/postinstall script is declared — and GYP evaluates `<!(...)` as a shell command during the configure step. The result is arbitrary command execution on the installer's machine on `npm install`, functionally identical to a lifecycle hook. The package is published as a Backstage backend plugin (pure TypeScript/JavaScript) and does not legitimately require a native addon; no C/C++ source files justify a binding.gyp. the analysis of the package contents was withheld by the provider safety filter (content_filtered stop reason), which itself indicates the traced content reads as operational malware. The combination of a content-filtered trace and a binding.gyp using command-expansion in a package with no native sources is the install-time-RCE fingerprint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
